articles.title: Small solar and battery specialist adds another 27 projects as it buys up competitor
articles.summary:
Sydney-based "distributed" renewables outfit inks deal to buy portfolio of 27 small solar and battery projects, to bolster its supply of "low‑cost and reliable" energy.
The post Small solar and battery specialist adds another 27 projects as it buys up competitor appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: Stunning growth of battery storage puts it at centre of global energy security needs, for cars and for grids
articles.summary:
The demand for batteries - for EVs and grid - has surged in the last year and is getting a further boost from the global fuel crisis as countries think energy security.
The post Stunning growth of battery storage puts it at centre of global energy security needs, for cars and for grids appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: Huge wind, solar and battery renewables project joins queue for federal green tick
articles.summary:
A massive hybrid renewables and battery project has joined the queue for federal environmental assessment, with state approvals already in hand.
The post Huge wind, solar and battery renewables project joins queue for federal green tick appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: Snowy elbows out Origin and others to land $1.9 billion renewable power deal for state’s trains and electric buses
articles.summary:
Updated: Snowy Hydro lands $1.9 billion deal to supply 100 pct renewable power for state's train, tram and electric buses, beating out others after a two-year tender process.
The post Snowy elbows out Origin and others to land $1.9 billion renewable power deal for state’s trains and electric buses appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: Energy Insiders Podcast: Batteries take centre stage as world wrestles with fuel crisis
articles.summary:
BMI's Iola Hughes discusses the extraordinary growth of battery storage, for EVs and the grid, their key role in energy security, and the different chemistries. Plus: Fortescue's fast-tracks path to real zero, finally some big wind, and will Albanese seize the moment?
The post Energy Insiders Podcast: Batteries take centre stage as world wrestles with fuel crisis appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: State threatens to step in if regulator and networks fail to remove EV charging “handbrakes” and sort out tariffs
articles.summary:
State says energy regulator and network companies must guarantee ready access to smarter and cheaper public electric vehicle charging, or face policy intervention.
The post State threatens to step in if regulator and networks fail to remove EV charging “handbrakes” and sort out tariffs appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: Transport for NSW inks $1.9 billion clean power deal with Snowy Energy
articles.summary: The New South Wales goverment has inked a seven year, $1.9 billion contract with federal government-owned Snowy Hydro retail arm Snowy Energy to power all public transport operations with renewable energy.

articles.title: Allume marks SolShare milestone connecting solar to 10,000 apartments
articles.summary: Victoria-based cleantech company Allume Energy has reached the significant milestone of connecting 10,000 apartments to solar energy in Australia, the UK, USA and Germany.

articles.title: Pacific nations call for 100% renewables, back fossil fuel non-proliferation treaty
articles.summary: On the back of state of emergencies recently declared across multiple Pacific nations, six island countries have united in a call for a fossil free Oceania and immediate transition to 100% renewable energy economies.

articles.title: Australia’s home energy success could be a global model in the midst of the energy crisis
articles.summary: With a volatile global fuel and energy crisis continuing relentlessly, Australia holds a quiet but powerful advantage that provides cost-of-living energy relief to millions by allowing our cars and homes to run on sunshine instead of fossil fuels.

articles.title: VIDEO – Energy Storage Summit 2026: Lessons learned from BESS construction projects
articles.summary: Building BESS is complicated, with myriad considerations around supplier contracts, site acceptance tests, data analysis, interface between different contractors.

articles.title: Sigenergy lists on Hong Kong Stock Exchange, raises HK$4.4 billion
articles.summary: On 16 April, battery storage solutions provider Sigenergy was listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange (HKEX) under the stock code 06656.HK.

articles.title: Hithium signs €400 million Spain battery gigafactory investment agreement after two-year courtship
articles.summary: Hithium has signed a formal investment commitment to establish a manufacturing facility in Spain's Navarre region.

articles.title: Akaysha inks deal with Copenhagen Energy for ‘mega-scale’ battery storage projects in Germany
articles.summary: Akaysha Energy has entered into a deal with Copenhagen Energy to advance “mega-scale” battery energy storage projects in Germany.

articles.title: Synergy submits 1GW Tathra Wind Farm with 500MW BESS to Australia’s EPBC Act
articles.summary: Synergy has submitted a hybrid renewable energy project to Australia's EPBC Act, featuring a 500MW battery energy storage system (BESS).

articles.title: Renewable generation records fall across the UK, as imported gas consumption tumbles
articles.summary:
As it continues to wean itself off fossil fuels, the UK has been setting new renewable energy generation records, while also pushing its share of imported energy to the lowest level since 2004.
The post Renewable generation records fall across the UK, as imported gas consumption tumbles appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: Budget test: Will Labor back a tax break to deliver cheap hot water to rentals?
articles.summary:
A modest tax tweak could unlock cheaper, cleaner energy for renters - and help them move away from gas - if the government is willing to act.
The post Budget test: Will Labor back a tax break to deliver cheap hot water to rentals? appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: Video: Flexible homes, flexible grid – Interview with Adam Cameron (SA Power Networks)
articles.summary:
At the EN2026 conference in Adelaide, SA Power Networks future grid engineer Adam Cameron explains how “smart homes” can help South Australia manage its world‑leading rooftop solar penetration. 
The post Video: Flexible homes, flexible grid – Interview with Adam Cameron (SA Power Networks) appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: State rolls out red carpet for four new wind projects in race to replace ageing coal generators
articles.summary:
Four wind farms and a green steel plant have been put on the development fast-track – the first to get priority treatment under new state legislation.
The post State rolls out red carpet for four new wind projects in race to replace ageing coal generators appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: Agrivoltaics maintain or enhance forage quality, study finds
articles.summary: Researchers have analyzed the biomass and nutritional value of grasses and legumes for grazing dairy cattle, growing in agrivoltaics fields. The results showed that forage quality may be maintained or even enhanced in agrivoltaic environments.

articles.title: Amazon commits to 430 MW of solar and BESS power purchase agreements
articles.summary: Amazon Australia has announced nine new power purchase agreements, that include three utility-scale solar and battery hybrids, four distributed solar-battery projects, and a new battery at the Mokoan Solar Farm, committing stable revenues to 430 MW of renewable energy.

articles.title: EVs with vehicle-to-home technology reduce need for large home batteries
articles.summary: Researchers from South and Western Australia have found electric vehicles equipped with vehicle-to-home technology can reduce the need for large home battery systems.

articles.title: 482 kWh neighbourhood battery powers community indoor cricket stadium
articles.summary: Victorian community-owned clean energy developer Indigo Power has launched a 482 kWh community battery with 100 kW inverter at the Cricket Albury Wodonga Indoor Stadium, co-located with an existing 98 kW solar system.

articles.title: CleanPeak Energy to acquire Sustainable Energy Infrastructure portfolio
articles.summary: Sydney-based renewable energy company CleanPeak Energy Holdings has agreed to acquire Adelaide-headquartered owner and developer of solar and battery assets Sustainable Energy Infrastructure.

articles.title: Free midday electricity key to drive consumer demand to match excess PV generation
articles.summary: Free midday electricity schemes aim to shift household demand into periods of high solar PV generation, reducing midday surplus and evening fossil-fuel ramp-up. Research on Australia’s Solar Sharer program suggests such incentives could significantly improve renewable utilisation, but outcomes depend on consumer behaviour, load shifting, and rebound effects.

articles.title: Puerto Rico approves aggregated residential energy storage motion from Tesla, Sunrun, and SunStrong
articles.summary: The Puerto Rico Energy Bureau (PREB) has approved a motion from Tesla, residential solar and battery installer Sunrun, and residential solar installer SunStrong Management to auto-enroll participants in an emergency capacity resource programme.

articles.title: Attracting infrastructure capital into large-scale battery storage: Eku Energy CEO Daniel Burrows
articles.summary: ESN Premium discusses "long-term value creation" for infrastructure investors, with Daniel Burrows of BESS developer Eku Energy.

articles.title: Strait of Hormuz closure creates complexity for global energy storage markets
articles.summary: Energy storage and battery market experts speak with Energy-Storage.news about current and possible supply chain and downstream impacts of the US and Israel's war on Iran.

articles.title: Virginia Governor Abigail Spanberger signs legislation mandating 20.78GW of energy storage
articles.summary: Virginia, US Governor Abigail Spanberger has signed legislation authorising the state to target  a total of 20.78GW of energy storage capacity.

articles.title: Investor Wood & Co engages partners to put 145MW of BESS into Nordic electricity markets
articles.summary: Czech investment group Wood & Company (Wood & Co) has hired construction and optimisation partners for large-scale battery storage projects in Finland and Sweden.

articles.title: VIDEO – Energy Storage Summit 2026: How attractive is BESS for M&A?
articles.summary: The attractiveness of the BESS market for M&A and investment, amidst a slowdown in activity, was discussed on a panel at the Energy Storage Summit 2026 in London.

articles.title: Neoen Australia submits 3.2GWh Bondo wind-plus-storage project to the EPBC Act
articles.summary: Neoen Australia has referred its 3,200MWh Bondo wind-plus-storage project for federal environmental assessment.

articles.title: Amazon Australia puts battery storage centre stage as it inks nine PPAs for data centre expansion
articles.summary: Amazon has announced nine new renewable energy PPAs in Australia totalling 430MW of capacity, eight of which include co-located BESS.

articles.title: Alinta Energy starts construction on 1,000MWh Stage 1 of South Australia’s Reeves Plains Battery
articles.summary: Alinta Energy has commenced main construction works on its 1,000MWh Stage 1 of its Reeves Plains BESS in South Australia.

articles.title: Victoria extends solar for apartments scheme to 30 June 2027
articles.summary: The Victorian Solar for Apartments program has been extended to 30 June 2027, giving applicants to the $16 million program an additional 14 months to secure grants.

articles.title: ICE moves ahead with modular green hydrogen production system
articles.summary: The company driving two of Australia’s largest renewable energy projects has announced key milestones for the system architecture that is to serve as the backbone for the proposed giga-scale green hydrogen projects.

articles.title: Dyness secures CEC approval for modular battery system
articles.summary: Chinese battery energy storage system manufacturer Dyness has expanded its available product portfolio in the Australian market with its Stack100 Pro securing the official tick of approval from the Clean Energy Council.

articles.title: Grid-forming inverters move mainstream as Australia seeks reliable, battery-ready infrastructure
articles.summary: Grid-forming inverters represent a critical technology transition for the Australian National Electricity Market as the grid moves toward 100% renewable sources. With 10 operational grid-forming battery energy storage systems totaling approximately 1,070 MW and 94 projects in the pipeline, Australia is emerging as a global leader in grid-forming deployment.

articles.title: UGL kicks off construction of six-hour battery project in WA
articles.summary: Western Australia’s energy transition has reached another milestone with construction of the 164 MW / 905 MWh Muchea battery project – the state’s first six-hour grid-scale battery – moving from “concept to reality.”

articles.title: VIDEO: ‘It’s going to massively scale here’: Fluence chief predicts data centre battery storage slingshot in Australia
articles.summary: Fluence’s chief growth officer, Jeff Monday, and Australia strategy lead, Sam Markham, discuss hyperscale opportunities, grid-forming evolution, and the company's high-density Smartstack deployment plans.

articles.title: ‘Unfettered optimism’ on US BESS degradation hits wall of operational reality
articles.summary: Industry leaders warned that "unfettered optimism" regarding battery degradation is hitting a wall of operational reality.

articles.title: Rye Development advances 2,128MWh pumped hydro project at former Kentucky coal mine
articles.summary: Developer Rye Development are partnering with Kentucky, US utility companies Louisville Gas and Electric (LG&E) and Kentucky Utilities (KU), on a 266MW/2,128MWh pumped hydro energy storage (PHES) project.

articles.title: VIDEO – Energy Storage Summit 2026: Battery recycling, lifecycle data and sustainability
articles.summary: How can the industry prepare for when the first wave of utility-scale BESS projects begin to reach end-of-life?

articles.title: British Gas owner Centrica activates 40MW BESS projects in Sweden, subsidiary to optimise
articles.summary: UK energy major Centrica has activated two BESS totalling 40MW in Borlänge, Sweden, expanding its European portfolio to more than 770MW.

articles.title: From ‘refrigerators in the desert’ to resilient, thermally passive BESS assets
articles.summary: Thermal management means cost, complexity and aux power consumption for battery storage projects, writes William Lauwers of Enertis Applus+.

articles.title: Fortescue fast-tracks ‘world’s largest off-grid system’ with 4-5GWh battery storage in Australia
articles.summary: Australian mining giant Fortescue has accelerated its timeline for deploying 4-5GWh of battery energy storage systems (BESS) paired with 1.8GW of renewable energy generation, which it now expects to begin commercial operation by 2028.

articles.title: UGL advances construction on 2.1GWh of battery storage for Neoen across two Australian states
articles.summary: Engineering firm UGL is progressing the construction of two battery energy storage systems (BESS) in Australia, totalling 2,125MWh.

articles.title: How solar farms shape local climate and vegetation in arid regions
articles.summary: Researchers have found that PV plants in arid regions create a measurable cool island effect that varies strongly with season, location, and plant design, influencing surrounding vegetation in complex and spatially uneven ways. They showed that cooling intensity and distance differ widely across sites, are driven mainly by plant morphology,

articles.title: Palisade inks deal to buy South Australian big battery project
articles.summary: One of the biggest batteries under construction in South Australia is set to change hands with Australian asset manager Palisade Investment Partners striking a deal to buy the 240 MW / 960 MWh Summerfield project from Copenhagen Infrastructure Partners.

articles.title: Halocell Energy, Sofab Inks advance perovskite collaboration
articles.summary: An ongoing collaboration between New South Wales-based Halocell Energy and Sofab Inks finds that perovskite modules incorporating Sofab’s Tinfab electron transport layer maintain approximately 100% of their normalised efficiency after 1,300 hours under accelerated combined light and damp-heat testing.

articles.title: Informa Markets brings Battery Asset Management Summit to Canada as energy storage market reaches critical deployment phase
articles.summary: Informa Markets is launching the inaugural Battery Asset Management Summit, Canada (BAM Canada).

articles.title: Europe deploys 3.4GWh of BESS in March, 19% of global total
articles.summary: Over 1.4GW/3.4GWh of grid-scale BESS came online in Europe, likely its best month ever, accounting for almost a fifth of global figures.

articles.title: Germany: NGEN starts building 100MWh BESS, Harmony Energy secures 3GWh pipeline financing
articles.summary: NGEN and Harmony Energy have made strides in deploying large-scale BESS projects in Germany, launching construction and securing pipeline financing respectively.

articles.title: Experts weigh in on navigating supply chain bottlenecks in US energy storage
articles.summary: A panel at the 2026 US Energy Storage Summit in Dallas, Texas, focused on the evolving landscape of battery energy storage system (BESS) procurement.

articles.title: Vietnam’s GG Power opens 5GWh BESS factory, government emphasises support for local supply chain
articles.summary: Vietnam’s government will “amend policies” to support production of clean energy technologies, a minister has said at the opening of a battery energy storage system (BESS) factory.

articles.title: Copenhagen Infrastructure Partners sells 960MWh Summerfield Battery to Palisade in South Australia
articles.summary: Copenhagen Infrastructure Partners has sold its 240MW/960MWh Summerfield Battery to Palisade Investment Partners in South Australia.

articles.title: 6 GW green hydrogen project for WA part of federal fast-track plans
articles.summary: Plans to build a green hydrogen production facility powered by up to 6 GW of solar and wind generation in Western Australia are moving forward with the project selected for accelerated development under the federal government’s new Investor Front Door pilot program.

articles.title: Solar leads global additions in 2025 as wind growth accelerates
articles.summary: A new report from global energy think tank Ember shows 814 GWdc in new solar and wind capacity was installed in 2025, but the pace of wind deployment rose 47% year-over-year compared to just 11% for solar.

articles.title: MyNu Energy launches mobile solar and battery energy storage system
articles.summary: MyNu Energy has launched a mobile solar generation and battery energy storage system designed to reduce reliance on diesel as the Middle East crisis continues to cause concerns about fuel shortages and uncertain energy costs.

articles.title: Fortescue fast-tracks delivery of ‘world first’ renewable energy grid
articles.summary: Australian mining giant Fortescue is fast tracking the delivery of what it says is the world’s first fully integrated green energy grid designed to completely eliminate diesel and other fossil fuels from large-scale industry.

articles.title: VIDEO: ‘Storage must come first’: Why Australia mandates parallel solar-plus-storage development but sequential commissioning
articles.summary: Envision's Behzad Naderi believes that Australia's hybridisation rules are reshaping development and connection processes across the NEM.

articles.title: ‘We’ll never be the cheapest’: Wärtsilä’s head of energy storage on company strategy
articles.summary: We catch up with Tamara de Gruyter, president of Wärtsilä Energy Storage, to discuss the system integrator's strategy under new leadership.

articles.title: Brazil’s electricity regulator ANEEL delays energy storage rulemaking, approves hybrid solar-BESS
articles.summary: Brazil's national regulator, ANEEL, postponed an important decision on energy storage, days after approving the country's first hybrid plant.

articles.title: System integrator Guoxia’s revenue and profits surge over 100% amidst shift to utility-scale
articles.summary: Energy storage firm Guoxia Technology (02655.HK) has released its first full annual report since listing on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange late last year.

articles.title: NSW planners approve Foresight’s 150MWh BESS; Banpu submits 1GWh development to Australia’s EPBC Act
articles.summary: A 150MWh system has been granted approval in Australia, whilst a 1,000MWh system has been submitted for federal consideration.

articles.title: Why system-level fire testing is becoming the new benchmark for grid-scale BESS safety
articles.summary: As utility-scale battery energy storage systems continue to be deployed across Australia’s National Electricity Market, fire risk assessment is rapidly shifting from a compliance exercise to a core project viability consideration. For developers, asset owners, insurance considerations and regulators, the key question is no longer whether systems meet component certification standards, but how complete BESS systems behave under severe real-world conditions.

articles.title: How LECO process could push TOPCon solar cell efficiency beyond 26%
articles.summary: A new UNSW study shows that laser-enhanced contact optimisation can boost industrial TOPCon solar cell efficiency by improving contact properties and reducing recombination losses. By combining optimised firing conditions with LECO “repair” of contacts, the approach balances recombination and resistance, offering a practical path for conventional TOPCon cells to compete with PV technologies offering higher efficiencies.

articles.title: Powering Australia’s next phase of decarbonisation: Why long-duration storage must lead
articles.summary: James Costello, CEO of EORA Energy, argues that long-duration vanadium redox flow battery storage is critical to Western Australia's decarbonisation efforts, particularly for remote mining operations.

articles.title: Photovoltaic research and innovation essential to ensure energy security
articles.summary: Researchers from 11 universities globally, including the University of New South Wales, have collaborated on a paper advocating for the need to guarantee energy security, research in photovoltaics is essential for continued innovation.

articles.title: Minnesota regulator approves Xcel’s controversial utility-owned virtual power plant
articles.summary: The Minnesota Public Utilities Commission (MPUC) has approved a 200MW expansion of utility Xcel Energy’s Capacity*Connect virtual power plant (VPP) programme.

articles.title: American Battery Factory, Lion Energy form partnership for US-made LFP BESS equipment
articles.summary: Lion Energy has announced a strategic partnership with gigafactory startup American Battery Factory to enable recently announced 4.5GWh offtake agreements.

articles.title: TenneT signs contract with developer for ‘first controllable congestion mitigator’ BESS project in Netherlands
articles.summary: Dutch TSO TenneT has signed a contract with a developer for a large-scale BESS which will help to relieve grid congestion, possibly enabling the quicker deployment of renewables in the Netherlands.

articles.title: Australia: Battery storage cushions AU$1 billion fuel shock as Climate Council demands May budget action
articles.summary: Australia's heavy reliance on imported fossil fuels has left households and businesses dangerously exposed to global energy shocks, with the current conflict involving the US and Iran costing motorists more than AU$1 billion (US$710 million) in March alone.

articles.title: ‘75% of what we need is not there’: Australian states race to deploy gigawatts of battery storage
articles.summary: New South Wales has been warned that it must accelerate the development of battery energy storage systems to meet its 2030 targets.

articles.title: Energy Vault the latest international player to tap Japan’s BESS market potential
articles.summary: Energy Vault has entered what it has described as “one of the world’s fastest-growing energy storage markets,” acquiring a portfolio of development projects in Japan.

articles.title: VIDEO – Energy Storage Summit 2026: What is the outlook for BESS Capex this year?
articles.summary: What does the combination of regulatory, supply chain and market pressures mean for the cost of building large-scale BESS this year?

articles.title: GridStor, Axpo execute energy storage revenue swap agreement for 440MWh Texas BESS
articles.summary: Gridstor and Axpo have executed an energy storage revenue swap agreement for a 220MW/440MWh BESS in Galveston County, Texas.

articles.title: VIDEO: European BESS integration with Chinese cells can be competitive, says Maxxen’s Ruben Valiente
articles.summary: Maxxen managing director Ruben Valiente speaks with Energy-Storage.news editor Andy Colthorpe at Energy Storage Summit 2026 in London.

articles.title: Beyond 314Ah: A comparison of large-format LFP battery cells for BESS
articles.summary: We analyse and compare the metrics of the various LFP battery cells of 587Ah and above, from leading providers CATL, Eve Energy, Hithium, Rept Battero, CALB and BYD.

articles.title: Engie buys 1.1GWh of synchronous condenser-paired BESS in Spain, starts building 220MWh France project
articles.summary: Multinational power firm Engie has acquired two large-scale BESS projects in Spain, paired with synchronous condensers and has launched construction on a BESS in France.

articles.title: EU-India trade agreement will propel innovation in energy storage
articles.summary: The recent European Union-India agreement could unlock innovation and scale in energy storage, writes Eugene Beh, CEO of Quino Energy.

articles.title: Private Energy Partners submits 780MW battery storage project to Australia’s EPBC Act
articles.summary: Private Energy Partners has submitted a hybrid energy project to Australia's environmental approval process, proposing a 780MW BESS.

articles.title: US battery storage developers get creative as pure merchant model fades
articles.summary: A panel at the 2026 US Energy Storage Summit in Dallas, Texas, discussed the “creative, innovative structures” developers are having to embrace to secure long-term revenues for energy storage projects.

articles.title: Canada’s smallest province, Prince Edward Island, issues REOI for up to 50MW of BESS
articles.summary: The Canadian Province of Prince Edward Island’s PEI Energy Corporation (PEIEC) has issued a request for expressions of interest (REOI) for a 10-50MW, four-hour duration battery energy storage system (BESS).

articles.title: IPPs Zelestra, BNZ adding BESS to solar PV plants in Southern Europe
articles.summary: IPPs Zelestra, BNZ and ALFI have secured offtake and financing to hybridise solar projects with BESS across Spain, Italy, Portugal and Romania.

articles.title: Home battery rebates and soaring diesel costs bring electrification to the masses
articles.summary:
What does it take to make a petrol-head tradie home electrification-curious? Try home battery rebates and soaring diesel costs.
The post Home battery rebates and soaring diesel costs bring electrification to the masses appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: A fair energy system is worth fighting for, but without playing the solar and battery blame game
articles.summary:
Claims that solar and battery households are “not paying their way” on the grid – and shifting costs to the have-nots – overstates the problem and misidentifies its cause.
The post A fair energy system is worth fighting for, but without playing the solar and battery blame game appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: Arena-backed green hydrogen plan disappears from queue for federal environmental approval
articles.summary:
Arena-backed project hoping to kickstart a major solar and wind-backed green hydrogen hub has been withdrawn from the federal environmental approvals process.
The post Arena-backed green hydrogen plan disappears from queue for federal environmental approval appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: Oversize and overmass: State allocates $180m to road upgrades to transport huge wind turbine parts
articles.summary:
State government tips more than $180 million into road network upgrades to support the transport of huge pieces of kit like wind turbine towers and blades.
The post Oversize and overmass: State allocates $180m to road upgrades to transport huge wind turbine parts appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: Electric trucks are increasingly competitive, and could be a thing – if only the transport minister would listen
articles.summary:
Updated: It is clear that electric trucks are increasingly competitive. Australia has an opportunity to seize the moment, but is the transport minister listening carefully enough?
The post Electric trucks are increasingly competitive, and could be a thing – if only the transport minister would listen appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: Energy Insiders Podcast: Is fuel rationing inevitable?
articles.summary:
How did it come to this? Energy security expert and former deputy chief of the RAAF John Blackburn on how Australia's has done nothing to prepare itself for the current fossil fuel crisis. Plus: EV sales are surging and electric trucks are a thing. Someone tell the transport minister!
The post Energy Insiders Podcast: Is fuel rationing inevitable? appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: Pacific Islands energy crisis: One country faces fuel import bills three times its healthcare budget
articles.summary:
Surging oil prices caused by overseas conflict has exposed energy vulnerabilities for Pacific islands and underscored the regional push towards renewables.
The post Pacific Islands energy crisis: One country faces fuel import bills three times its healthcare budget appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: State-owned transmission giant wants to claw back 45 pct more revenue from customers, citing “increased complexity”
articles.summary:
The state-owned network wants to hike its revenue base and add that to customer bills, just as the state government crows about pushing power costs down. 
The post State-owned transmission giant wants to claw back 45 pct more revenue from customers, citing “increased complexity” appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: AGL reaches FID on new peaking plant that will just about pay for itself without ever being switched on
articles.summary:
Australia's biggest coal generator commits to a new peaking power station that will likely recoup its investment in eight years, even if it's not switched on.
The post AGL reaches FID on new peaking plant that will just about pay for itself without ever being switched on appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: ClearVue secures global certification for metal-backed solar panels
articles.summary: Building-integrated PV technology company ClearVue Technologies is a step closer to commercial deployment of its metal-backed solar panels after securing global IEC certification for the integrated rooftop system.

articles.title: Trucking company says fully electric delivery ushers in new era for road freight
articles.summary: Trucking company New Energy Transport says electric road freight is possible now and already commercially viable in Australia after completing what it is calling the country’s first fully electric ‘end-to-end’ heavy road freight delivery.

articles.title: Sungrow launches two energy storage solutions for the Australian market
articles.summary: The PowerTitan 3.0 and Hybrid Solution systems are designed to meet the needs of the rapidly-expanding utility-scale renewable energy sector in Australia.

articles.title: Harmony starts building third 100MW/200MWh French BESS
articles.summary: BESS developer-operator Harmony Energy has started construction on a 100MW/200MWh BESS in France, its third to reach that stage in the country.

articles.title: UL9540A: Shift to system-level testing defines new edition of key BESS safety standard
articles.summary: ESN Premium discusses the sixth edition of the key industry safety standard, UL9540A, with Dana Parmenter of the standards organisation and NRTL, CSA Group.

articles.title: Japan’s first capacity market-contracted BESS brought online by Hexa Energy
articles.summary: Developer-investor Hexa Energy Services has inaugurated what is thought to be Japan’s first operational battery storage project with a capacity market contract.
